claim: Birkhoff 1930 (Acta Math 54, slot 01) introduces the linear-difference / linear-ODE operator L(y) = a_0(x) y(x+n) + a_1(x) y(x+n-1) + ... + a_n(x) y(x) = 0 and asserts the existence of a 'full quota of formal series (normal and anormal) for each singular point' (page_idx 0, 3) — canonical L-equation framing for the PCF context.

Birkhoff-Trjitzinsky 1933 (Acta Math 60, slot 03) §1 + Theorem 9 (Fundamental Existence Theorem) treat a linear difference system y_i(x+1) = Σ_j a_ij(x) y_j(x) of order n and reduce it to a single equation L_n(y) = 0 (page_idx 0-1) with formal-series solutions e^{Q_j(x)} · s_j(x) (page_idx 51, eq. 2a) — refereed-rigorous L-equation framing in the PCF discrete-recurrence context.

Costin 2008 chapter 5 (slot 06) is titled 'Ordinary differential equations. Summability of formal solutions' (ToC page_idx 5; chapter starts page_idx 149); Theorem 5.11 (page_idx 151) describes the analytic continuation in the Borel plane of the formal-series solutions of an ODE — canonical L-equation+Borel-summability framing.

Wasow 1965 PDF (slot 04) is image-only: pypdf returns zero extractable text across all 34 pages; consistent with prior Q20A-PHASE-C-RESUME 2026-05-03 finding (image-only halt). Wasow §VIII §34 / §IX-X L-equation framing is informationally absent from this slot but replaceable by Birkhoff-Trjitzinsky 1933 + Costin 2008 ch.5 for the canonical-statement goal.

Okamoto 1987 (Funkcialaj Ekvacioj 30, slot 07) §1 introduces P_III' / P_III as a Hamiltonian system in (q, p) with Hamiltonian H_III'(t; q, p), via dq/dt = ∂H/∂p, dp/dt = -∂H/∂q (page_idx 1-3, eqs. P_III, 0.3) — NOT as a linear ODE in (z, w); group G_* of birational canonical transformations is isomorphic to the affine Weyl group W(B_2) (page_idx 0).

Okamoto 1987 references [5] = Jimbo-Miwa 'Monodromy preserving deformation of linear ordinary differential equations with rational coefficients II' (Physica 2D, 1981) (page_idx 27 references list) — the explicit pointer to the Lax-pair / monodromy-preserving-deformation construction that BLMP 2024 §4.1 reproduces.

Barhoumi-Lisovyy-Miller-Prokhorov 2024 (SIGMA 20, slot 08) §4.1 (page_idx 23) gives the canonical textbook statement 'Following Jimbo and Miwa, we use the fact that each Painlevé equation can be recast as an isomonodromic deformation condition for a 2×2 system of linear ODEs with rational coefficients'; the Lax pair (Λ(λ, x), X(λ, x)) is explicit with compatibility condition ∂Λ/∂x − ∂X/∂λ + [Λ, X] = 0 (page_idx 24, eq. above 4.4) — refereed-rigorous canonical statement of the L-equation vs isomonodromic-deformation layer separation.

Synthesis (Phase C.1, handoff.md): the L-equation is a linear ODE / linear-difference operator L(y) = 0 satisfied by the OGF / wave function in the (z, w)-function-space layer (Birkhoff 1930, B-T 1933, Costin 2008 ch.5); the isomonodromic deformation lives in the (q, p, t)-Hamiltonian / monodromy-data layer (Okamoto 1987, BLMP 2024 §4.1 after Jimbo-Miwa 1981 II); the Lax pair (Λ, X) is the bridge, with compatibility condition [∂_x − X, ∂_λ − Λ] = 0 returning P_III(D_6) as the deformation equation; CT v1.3 §3.5 'algebraic identity at Painlevé-class level only' wording is implicit-only and explicit amendment is recommended for CT v1.4 §3.5. Verdict UPGRADE_G17_LIT_ANCHOR_FOUND_AMENDMENT_RECOMMENDED.
